methodsAttempted.query: site:rug.nl University of Groningen generative AI policy teaching privacy le chat
methodsAttempted.notes: Returned central policy pages, EDU Support guidance, AI Office tools, and two non-policy exploratory pages.

methodsAttempted.query: site:rug.nl University of Groningen AI policy teaching fraud Le Chat FAQ
methodsAttempted.notes: Confirmed the main guidance pages directly on rug.nl and edusupport.rug.nl.
Expanded into AI Office, education quality assurance, and EDU Support path families.

artifacts.summary: Official central guidance pages were found for safe AI use, teaching rules, fraud and assessment risks, AI Office tools, and Le Chat FAQ data rules. Two exploratory pages were rejected because they were informative but not binding policy sources.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: The Community of Practice A.I. in Education focuses on exploring the implications of artificial intelligence in education.
artifacts.notes: Exploratory community page with events and best practices, not binding university policy.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: AI in Academia - Smart, but Not Very Green is a sustainability blog about AI's environmental impact and student habits.
artifacts.notes: Editorial sustainability blog, not a policy or guidance page.

artifacts.claimText: University of Groningen's AI policy and regulations page tells UG users not to share personal or organizational details with AI systems, to keep human intervention for decisions about other people, and to follow GDPR and related laws.
artifacts.normalizedValue: do not share personal or organizational details; keep human intervention; follow GDPR

artifacts.claimText: University of Groningen's teaching AI policy says AI tools may be used as aids, GenAI use beyond that must be mentioned, and faculties or courses may add extra rules.
artifacts.normalizedValue: AI aids allowed; GenAI use must be cited; extra faculty/course rules may apply

artifacts.sourceTitle: UG basic rules for the use of AI in teaching
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: AI tools may be used as aids. When GenAI functionalities are used, this should always be mentioned/referred to. Additional rules may apply at the level of your faculty, degree programme, and course unit.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: UG allows AI tools as aids, requires GenAI use to be mentioned, and notes that extra faculty or course rules may apply.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Ten UG basic rules for the use of AI in teaching

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The unauthorized use of AI tools by students that hinders the assessment of a learning goal can be considered fraud and a breach of academic integrity. Cheating scores that are generated by AI detection tools may not be used to prove that a student has cheated.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: UG guidance treats unauthorized student AI use that harms assessment as possible fraud and rejects AI-detection scores as proof.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Le Chat (Mistral AI) is a secure and reliable AI assistant for the UG that can help with daily tasks. The pilot offers the opportunity to familiarize yourself with AI within a safe and experimental environment.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: UG describes Le Chat as a secure, reliable assistant and frames the rollout as a pilot in a safe experimental setting.

artifacts.title: University of Groningen staged AI policy evidence draft
artifacts.summary: Staged OpenClaw crawl for University of Groningen found official central guidance on safe AI use, teaching rules, academic integrity, the UG AI Office Le Chat pilot, and Le Chat FAQ data-handling rules. Two exploratory pages from the Teaching Academy community page and a sustainability blog were rejected because they were informative but not binding policy sources.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The central policy pages support broad guidance, but any faculty, degree programme, or course unit can impose additional rules.
Exploratory community and blog pages were rejected and were not used for binding policy claims.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
